name: Shift handover notes
current_process: At each shift change (typically 2–3 times per day), the outgoing shift team hands over critical information to the incoming team. The handover usually takes place in the office or a private area and lasts 10–40 minutes depending on the home and the number of events during the shift. The outgoing senior carer or nurse summarises key events for each resident: changes in condition, falls or incidents, medication changes, GP or hospital visits, family concerns, behavioural observations, food and fluid intake, and any outstanding tasks. This information is communicated verbally and documented in a handwritten handover sheet or communication book. Some homes use the SBAR framework (Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation) for structured handovers, though many rely on unstructured verbal summaries. In some homes, handover involves physically visiting each resident's room and communal areas. Paper handover notes must remain on-site for confidentiality. Staff often compile handover notes in the final 15–30 minutes of their shift, pulling information from daily care notes, observation charts, and memory. In some homes, staff are not paid for handover time, creating pressure to rush. Key risks include information being lost between shifts, inconsistent detail depending on who is handing over, and important observations being forgotten if not documented promptly.
time_spent: Estimated 1–2 hours per day across the care team (factoring in 2–3 handovers daily). Each handover meeting takes 15–40 minutes. Preparing handover notes in the final portion of a shift takes an additional 15–30 minutes per staff member. In nursing research, nurse handoffs take an average of 40 minutes per shift; across large organisations this aggregates to millions of hours annually. For a typical 40-bed care home with 2 handovers per day, the total time commitment is approximately 7–14 hours per week when preparation, delivery, and documentation time are combined.
ai_solution: AI can transform handover from a memory-dependent, rushed process into a comprehensive, accurate, and efficient transfer of care: (1) Auto-generated handover summaries — AI pulls data from the shift's digital care notes, observation entries, incident reports, and medication records to automatically compile a structured handover report for each resident, highlighting changes, concerns, and outstanding actions. Staff review rather than write from scratch. (2) Voice-to-text capture — staff dictate observations throughout the shift using a mobile device, and AI structures these into care notes and handover-ready summaries in real time, eliminating the end-of-shift documentation rush. (3) Priority flagging — AI highlights the most critical items (deteriorating NEWS2 scores, unresolved incidents, medication changes, family complaints) so the incoming team knows exactly where to focus. (4) Continuity tracking — AI monitors whether actions flagged in previous handovers have been completed, preventing items from falling through the cracks across multiple shift changes. (5) SBAR-structured outputs — AI automatically formats handover information using the SBAR framework for clinical consistency. HCA Healthcare (US) has deployed Google Cloud AI-powered Nurse Handoff, an app that generates patient summaries from electronic records, demonstrating the model at scale.
buyer_language: "Handover is always rushed — the day shift is trying to get home and the night staff are still putting their coats away. Half the time something important gets missed because it wasn't written down, or the notes are so scribbled you can't read them. We need to know what actually happened, not just what someone remembers to tell us."
outcome_statement: Handover summaries generated automatically from the shift's care records — accurate, complete, and ready in seconds, so nothing falls through the cracks between shifts.
regulatory_alignment: Maps to CQC Quality Statement: 'Safe systems, pathways and transitions' under the Safe key question — ensuring safe and effective handover of care information between shifts, teams, and services. Also aligns with 'How staff and teams work together' (Effective) — effective communication between team members is essential for continuity of care. Relevant to 'Monitoring and improving outcomes' (Effective) for tracking resident changes across shifts. NICE guideline NG94 recommends structured handovers using standardised tools and protected handover time.
existing_tech_gap: Care management platforms (Nourish, Person Centred Software, Log my Care) capture digital care notes throughout the shift but do not auto-generate handover summaries from those notes. CareVision offers a digital handover tool that consolidates care notes into a handover view, but it is essentially a filtered display rather than an AI-generated summary. ShiftCare offers AI voice-to-text that converts verbal records into structured summaries with incident flagging. HCA Healthcare's Nurse Handoff app (US, built with Google Cloud) demonstrates AI-generated patient summaries from EHR data at scale. No mainstream UK care home platform currently offers fully AI-generated shift handover summaries with priority flagging, action tracking, and SBAR formatting. AI adds unique value by synthesising an entire shift's worth of care data into a concise, prioritised, clinically relevant summary.
implementation_complexity: Requires integration — AI handover generation needs access to the care home's digital care notes system (Nourish, PCS, or similar) to pull shift data. Voice-to-text capture requires mobile devices and reliable internet connectivity throughout the home. The transition from verbal/paper handover to AI-assisted handover requires cultural change and staff training. Homes already using digital care recording will find implementation simpler than those still on paper.
proof_of_concept_availability: Partial. HCA Healthcare's Nurse Handoff app (US) is a working, deployed product that uses Google Cloud AI to generate patient summaries for shift handover — demonstrating the concept at scale across 186 hospitals. ShiftCare (available in UK) offers AI voice-to-text that generates structured summaries from verbal records. CareVision offers a digital handover tool for UK care homes. Academic research (medRxiv, Wiley) has validated AI-powered handover note generation in clinical settings. No fully productised UK care home-specific AI handover solution exists yet as a turnkey product.
